text: Youth sport specialisation is not required to achieve success at elite levels of sport
Elite athletes typically specialised in their sport between ages 14 and 15, later than non-elite or semi-elite peers who specialised prior to age 13
The proportion of elite athletes who specialised early in youth ranged between 7% and 85%, varying by sport and definition of specialisation
Youth sport specialisation does not improve task-related performance outcomes such as neuromuscular control or anterior reach compared with non-specialised athletes
Later sport specialisation (after age 13) is associated with reaching elite status, suggesting early diversification may benefit long-term athletic development
